relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Moses fears the people will not trust the encounter.
YHWH begins with what is already in Moses' hand.
The staff becomes serpent, and Moses flees.
What frightened him becomes staff again when grasped by command.
The sign speaks: the ancestral Divine has appeared.
The hand becomes afflicted and visible as vulnerability.
The hand is restored to flesh.
If one sign is not heard, another may speak.
Even Nile water can become witness on dry ground.
Moses names his heavy mouth and heavy tongue.
YHWH answers as maker of mouth, hearing, and sight.
Presence will be with Moses' mouth.
Moses still asks to be spared the sending.
Aaron is given as joyful support.
Words will be shared between mouths.
Aaron becomes mouth; Moses carries Divine commission.
The staff becomes sign-bearing instrument.
Jethro releases Moses in peace.
The danger from Egypt has shifted.
Moses returns with family and staff of Divine.
Signs are placed in Moses' hand, but Pharaoh's heart will harden.
Israel is named YHWH's firstborn son.
Refusing release brings firstborn confrontation.
An obscure night-danger interrupts the journey.
Zipporah acts with flint and bloods to preserve life.
The danger passes, but the saying remains mysterious.
Aaron meets Moses at the mountain and kisses him.
Moses gives Aaron the words and signs.
The elders of Israel are gathered.
Aaron speaks and signs are shown.
The people trust, hear that YHWH has visited them, bow, and worship.

contemplative_note: Exodus 4 is reluctant calling with accommodation: signs in the hand, words shared through Aaron, Zipporah's fierce intervention, and a people who finally hear that YHWH has seen them.
